title: 10 best herbal tinctures for enlarged spleen
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for enlarged spleen are concentrated liquid extracts derived from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have been traditionally used to support spleen function and reduce its enlargement. These tinctures help by stimulating the spleen's natural filtering process, promoting healthy circulation, and reducing inflammation. Examples of herbal tinctures that may be beneficial for enlarged spleen include Turmeric, Ginger, and Dandelion root, which can improve overall health and quality of life. By using these tinctures, individuals with enlarged spleens may experience reduced pain, improved digestion, and enhanced energy levels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because its unique blend of compounds, particularly adiantum, has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote liver detoxification. The tincture's antioxidant properties help to soothe the liver and kidneys, which in turn can alleviate pressure on the spleen, reducing its enlargement. Additionally, Maidenhair tree tinctures may also help to improve blood flow and circulation, further contributing to a healthy spleen function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to spleen enlargement by decreasing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen.

Immunomodulatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help regulate inflammation and promote a balanced immune response, potentially reducing symptoms of an enlarged spleen.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation in the spleen and alleviate symptoms associated with enlarged spleen.

Terpenoids: Specifically, ginkgolides and bilobalide, these terpenoids may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the spleen, which could contribute to the alleviation of symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.

Bilobalide: As a sesquiterpene trilactone, bilobalide may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the spleen from damage and reduce inflammation, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to their medicinal properties.
Seeds: The seeds contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, compounds thought to help with inflammation and blood flow regulation.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate spleen enlargement symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the ginkgo biloba mixture in the jar with 70 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio by volume.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding solids.
Store the ginkgo biloba tincture in the fridge to slow down the aging process, good for 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with the condition. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to inhibit inflammation-causing enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the liver and spleen from oxidative damage, promoting overall health and well-being. As a natural and effective remedy, turmeric tinctures can provide relief and support for individuals struggling with an enlarged spleen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the spleen, which can alleviate discomfort and symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the spleen from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and enlargement of the organ.
Immunomodulatory property: Turmeric's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune response and reduce excessive inflammation in the spleen, thereby helping to shrink an enlarged spleen.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the spleen, which can contribute to its enlargement.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It has anti-inflammatory properties and may help prevent the growth of abnormal cells in the spleen, reducing its size.
Tumerone: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate the underlying causes of spleen enlargement, such as infection or autoimmune disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are rich in curcuminoids, which have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve overall health.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Curcuma longa also contain volatile oils, which contribute to their medicinal properties and can help soothe spleen-related issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh rhizomes of curcuma longa when the tops begin to yellow and the leaves drop.
Clean and chop the harvested rhizomes into small pieces weighing about 500 grams.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 70 percent ethanol in a ratio of 1:3 by weight to make the tincture.
Store the mixture in a clean glass container and let it steep in a cool dark place for two weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because its bioactive compounds, particularly allicin, exhibit potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds have been shown to reduce splenomegaly by decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress in the spleen. Additionally, garlic's antimicrobial and antifungal properties help combat infections that can contribute to an enlarged spleen. As a natural and effective remedy, herbal garlic tinctures work to alleviate symptoms of splenomegaly, promoting overall liver health and functioning.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Garlic's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial and fungal overgrowth, which can contribute to inflammation and enlargement of the spleen.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in garlic, such as allicin, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the spleen, thereby alleviating its enlargement.

Antioxidant properties: Garlic's antioxidant properties help to protect the spleen from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and enlargement of the organ.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur-containing compound found in Allium sativum, may help reduce spleen enlargement by exerting anti-inflammatory properties, which could alleviate the underlying conditions contributing to spleen enlargement.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, may help reduce spleen enlargement by modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, which could help alleviate conditions such as splenomegaly (enlarged spleen) associated with infections or autoimmune diseases.

S-Allyl cysteine sulfoxide (SAC): SAC, a compound found in Allium sativum, may help reduce spleen enlargement by exerting antioxidant properties, which could help protect the spleen from oxidative damage and alleviate conditions contributing to spleen enlargement.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used because they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds responsible for garlic's medicinal properties.
Roots: They are used because they contain essential oils and sulfur compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
Leaves: They are used because they contain a significant amount of flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh allium sativum roots and wash them thoroughly with cool water to remove dirt.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a food processor with a chopping attachment.
Combine the chopped roots with 3 cups of 95% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with enlarged spleen because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the rhizomes with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of boswellia serrata resin and clean it thoroughly with a fine mesh strainer.
